user_message: I need to prep a brief for the Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 15:12 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 11:41 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 09:39 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-02-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:14:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:19:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - migration architecture

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
